resources.string: Uses dual cameras when available; falls back to rear camera if unsupported or downgraded.
The current resolution, frame rate, or bitrate exceeds the safe dual-camera profile; recording will fall back to rear-only mode.
This device currently supports rear-camera recording only.
Always uses the rear camera for lower power use and better stability.
Always uses the front camera for cabin-facing capture; front mirroring can be controlled separately.
No usable front camera is currently detected, so Front only is unavailable.
Profile exceeds the safe dual-camera tier
Camera permission is required to start dashcam recording.
Notification permission is required to reliably show background recording status.
Ambient driving audio is enabled; the next rear-camera recording will include audio.
Microphone permission was denied; silent recording remains enabled.
Location permission was denied; GPS location and route logging stays off.
Location permission granted; emergency events can record the latest available coordinates.
Location permission was denied; emergency events still record time, trigger type, and linked clips.
Bluetooth permission granted; trusted Bluetooth connections can now auto-start recording.
Bluetooth permission was denied; trusted Bluetooth auto-start is unavailable.
Shows dashcam recording status and a quick stop action.
Stop recording before changing the mode.
Changes are locked during recording and apply after stop.
When enabled, front-camera preview and front-camera video use mirrored output.
This setting applies whenever the front camera is active. If Auto falls back to rear only, mirroring is ignored.
Rear-only mode does not use the front camera, so front mirroring is unavailable.
Follow system uses the current screen orientation. Fixed landscape driving prioritizes landscape layout and video orientation.
In Auto mode, the safe dual-camera profile tops out at 1080p / 30fps / 12Mbps. Higher combinations automatically fall back to rear only.
Target resolution; automatically falls back to a lower preset if the device or dual-camera setup cannot support it.
Target frame rate; CameraX will try to match it, but the final result still depends on device and concurrent recording capability.
Higher target bitrate usually improves quality, but also increases heat, power use, and storage consumption.
Range %1$d-%2$dGB, about 80%% of currently available space.
Off by default; asks for microphone permission when enabled, and writes ambient audio to rear/single-camera clips only.
Front-only mode keeps video silent even if ambient audio is enabled. Use Rear only or Auto if you need audio.
Enabled by default; automatically falls back to rear-only recording when severe overheating is detected.
Forced off; overheating will not automatically disable the front camera.
Disable front camera on low battery
Enabled by default; automatically falls back to rear-only recording when battery is low and the device is not charging.
Forced off; low battery will not automatically disable the front camera.
Disable front camera when segment switching is slow
Enabled by default; automatically downgrades when segment switching gets too slow to prioritize continuous rear recording.
Forced off; high segment-switch pressure will not automatically disable the front camera.
Off by default; when enabled, emergency events save location and recent route data. When off, no location metadata is recorded.
Allowed; location and recent route data are saved only after location permission is granted.
Off by default; location permission is required before enabling it, but core recording still works when disabled.
Off by default; generates SRT sidecar subtitles during evidence export so watermarks can be previewed in external players.
Off by default; evidence export creates transcoded copies with burned-in watermarks while keeping original clips unchanged.
Auto-start recording when power is connected
Starts foreground recording when power is connected; requires camera and notification permissions.
Enter the car Bluetooth name or MAC address for exact-match auto start.
Auto-start recording on trusted Bluetooth connection
Starts automatically when the specified Bluetooth device connects; requires camera, notification, and Bluetooth permissions.
Estimated remaining recording time with current settings: %1$s
Grant Bluetooth permission to select from paired devices.
No paired devices are currently readable. You can still enter the car Bluetooth name or MAC address manually.
The most recent dual-camera recording fell back to rear-only mode. Check it together with the notification status.
Stop recording before changing the recording mode.
Switched to Auto: uses dual cameras when available, and falls back to rear camera if unsupported or downgraded.
Switched to Auto: the current profile exceeds the safe dual-camera tier, so recording will fall back to rear only.
Switched to Auto: this device currently supports rear-camera recording only.
Switched to Rear only: recording will always use the rear camera.
Switched to Front only: recording will always use the front camera.
No usable front camera is available, so Front only cannot be selected.
Stop recording before changing frame rate.
Stop recording before changing segment duration.
Stop recording before changing collision sensitivity.
Stop recording before changing ambient audio.
Switched to silent recording; future clips will not contain an audio track.
Ambient driving audio is enabled; audio is written to rear/single-camera clips only.
Ambient audio is enabled, but Front only clips stay silent. Switch to Rear only or Auto if you need audio.
Front camera mirroring is enabled for preview and front-camera video.
Orientation strategy set to Follow system.
Orientation strategy set to Fixed landscape driving.
Thermal performance guard enabled; severe overheating will automatically disable the front camera and prioritize rear recording.
Thermal performance guard disabled; overheating will not automatically disable the front camera.
Low-battery performance guard enabled; the front camera will automatically turn off when battery is low and the device is not charging.
Low-battery performance guard disabled; low battery will not automatically disable the front camera.
Segment-switch performance guard enabled; the front camera will automatically turn off if segment gaps become too long.
Segment-switch performance guard disabled; segment-switch pressure will not automatically disable the front camera.
Evidence export will include timestamp/speed watermark subtitles for easier preview in external players.
Evidence export will create burned-in timestamp/speed/location watermark video copies while keeping original clips unchanged.
Burned-in watermark video export is disabled; evidence packages will continue to include the original clips.
Auto-start on power connected is enabled; camera and notification permissions are required in advance.
Auto-start on power connected is disabled.
Enter the trusted Bluetooth device name or MAC address first.
Auto-start on trusted Bluetooth connection is enabled; camera, notification, and Bluetooth permissions are required in advance.
Auto-start on trusted Bluetooth connection is disabled.
Emergency lock sent: the current, previous, and next segments will be protected.

This will restore the default recording mode, resolution, frame rate, bitrate, storage, audio, performance guard, GPS, watermark export, and auto-start settings. Recorded clips, locked events, and exported evidence packages will not be deleted.
The target limit of %1$dGB is lower than the current normal clip usage of %2$s. Confirming will immediately delete the oldest normal clips. Locked clips will not be deleted.
At least %1$s needs to be freed.
This only deletes the event record and evidence metadata. It does not delete the linked clip files.
Confirming deletes the local video file and removes its reference from emergency event records.
No emergency events yet. Manual locks and collision triggers will appear here with the trigger time and linked clips.
No clips yet. Once recording starts, recent normal and locked videos will appear here.
No clips match the current filters.
This group is missing that camera clip. Export the available clip first if needed.
Original files stay untouched. Export a single camera file or package both files for evidence.
This clip group is missing or has already been removed.
The rear clip is missing, so rear-only export is unavailable.
The front clip is missing, so front-only export is unavailable.
Could not save the exported file. Please try again.
Auto sync: corrected previous %1$dms offset, current offset %2$dms
Grant camera permission to show the rear preview
Front inset is temporarily unavailable, falling back to rear preview
Dual-camera preview requires an Activity lifecycle
This event has no available coordinates
No map app was found to open these coordinates.
No app was found that can play MP4 files.
Rear-camera MP4 recording is ready. Dual-camera recording is still being integrated in the next phase.
Rear recording service started. Preview and recording share the CameraX pipeline with %1$d-minute segments.
The recording storage limit is lower than current normal clip usage. Confirm before applying it.
%1$dGB recording storage applied, removed %2$d normal clips, freed %3$s.
%1$dGB recording storage applied; no normal clips needed cleanup.
Removed %1$d normal clips and freed %2$s; locked clips were not affected.
Normal clips are still within the %1$dGB limit; no cleanup needed.
Defaults restored; recordings, emergency events, and exported evidence packages were not deleted.
GPS location and route logging enabled; future emergency events will save the latest location and route.
GPS location and route logging disabled; future emergency events will not save location or route data.
This clip is already a normal clip.
Unlocked: %1$s. This clip will now follow loop-storage management.
The clip does not exist or is outside the managed directory.
Emergency event record deleted; linked clips remain in history.
Emergency event clip links are intact; no repair needed.
Repaired %1$d emergency events, removed %2$d invalid clip references; %3$d events currently have no available clips.
Unable to open emergency event clips: %1$s
Another export is already running. Please wait.
Export cancelled. Original recordings were not affected.
Writing segment %1$d from both rear and front cameras
Writing segment %1$d; this session has fallen back to rear-only recording
Emergency lock triggered. The current and previous segments are protected, and the next completed segment will be locked too.
Dual-camera startup failed, fell back to rear-only recording: %1$s
There is no active segment to lock
Possible collision detected at %1$sg, creating emergency event
Possible collision detected at %1$sg, executing emergency lock
No usable accelerometer was provided by the device. Automatic collision lock is unavailable.
GPS location and route logging enabled
GPS location and route logging disabled
Performance guard triggered: front camera disabled, rear recording continues (%1$s)
Device is overheating and the front camera must be disabled to reduce heat.
Battery is below %1$d%% and the front camera must be disabled to extend rear recording.
Segment switching is too slow and the front camera must be disabled to reduce encoder pressure.
Device temperature is rising; continuing dual-camera recording may trigger automatic downgrade.
Battery is below %1$d%%. Connect power if possible.
%1$s triggered auto start. Tap to begin recording.
%1$s triggered auto start. Android 14+ does not allow camera recording to begin directly from the background, so tap “Start recording”.
Shows auto-start recording requests that require user confirmation
No trusted Bluetooth device is configured.
The connection broadcast did not include Bluetooth device information.
The connected device does not match the trusted device.
Trusted Bluetooth connected and recording started.
Power connection broadcast received and recording started.
Android 14+ restricts background camera recording start. A start prompt was shown instead.
The system blocked background foreground-service startup.
The system blocked background recording startup because it would use camera, microphone, or location permissions.
Camera permission is required before capability checks and recording can run.
A complete rear/front camera pair was not detected.
The system version is below Android 11, so concurrent rear/front capability cannot be determined reliably.
This device reports support for concurrent rear and front cameras.
This device does not report support for concurrent rear and front cameras.
Camera permission is not granted, so recording cannot start.
There is no current recording segment to lock.
Failed to lock the current segment.
Failed to switch to the next recording segment.
Camera permission is not granted, so dual-camera recording cannot start.
Dual-camera recording failed to start and fell back to rear-only recording: %1$s
Camera permission is not granted, so the dual-camera preview cannot be shown.
Unable to save the evidence package.
The locked clip does not exist.
